body:
🟡 **Sidebar 'Genel Bakış' (root `/`) is always highlighted as active for every route** The sidebar navigation item "Genel Bakış" with `href: "/"` is always shown as active regardless of which page the user is on.
Root Cause At `components/layout/sidebar.tsx:43`, the `isActive` check is: ```js const isActive = pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`); ``` For the root item with `href: "/"`, `pathname.startsWith("/")` is **always true** for every route (e.g. `/crm/leads`, `/performance`, etc.). This means "Genel Bakış" will always have the active styling (`bg-primary/10 text-primary`) applied simultaneously with whatever other nav item is truly active. **Impact:** The sidebar always highlights "Genel Bakış" in addition to the actual active page, confusing users about their current location.
*(Refers to line 43)* --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🔴 **Import script embeds `` tags inside every generated page component** Every generated page component contains nested ``, ``, and `` tags rendered inside a React component's JSX. These are nested inside the root layout's own `` and `` tags (defined in `app/layout.tsx:23-40`), producing invalid HTML with duplicate document structure elements.
Root Cause in import-html.ts At `import-html.ts:86-88`, the script does: ```js const $main = cheerio.load(mainContent); $main('header').first().remove(); mainContent = $main.html(); ``` `cheerio.load(mainContent)` wraps the fragment in `...` structure. Then `$main.html()` returns the full document including those wrapper tags. The script should have used `$main('body').html()` or `$main.root().html()` with appropriate options to avoid the document wrapper. **Impact:** All 9 generated page files contain `...` nested inside a `
`, which is nested inside the root layout's ``. This produces invalid HTML in every new route. While browsers may be somewhat tolerant of this, it can cause unpredictable rendering issues, break accessibility tools, and violate the HTML spec.
```suggestion const $main = cheerio.load(mainContent, { xmlMode: false }, false); $main('header').first().remove(); mainContent = $main.html(); ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🔴 **SVG gradient `` style attributes are dropped, rendering gradient invisible** The SVG `` `` elements in the performance page have `style={{}}` (empty objects), meaning no `stop-color` or `stop-opacity` is applied. The gradient fill area under the chart line will be invisible.
Root Cause in import-html.ts style conversion At `import-html.ts:39-54`, the `htmlToJsx` function handles `style` attributes with a regex. It only recognizes `background-image` and `width` CSS properties. For any other CSS property (like `stop-color: #136dec; stop-opacity: 1`), it falls through to the catch-all `return 'style={{}}'` at line 53, discarding the style entirely. The original HTML likely had: ```html ``` But the output at `app/(dashboard)/performance/page.tsx:80-81` is: ```jsx ``` **Impact:** The gradient area fill in the productivity chart is completely transparent/invisible instead of showing a colored gradient.
Prompt for agents ``` In import-html.ts, the htmlToJsx function's style attribute handler (lines 39-54) only recognizes background-image and width CSS properties. All other styles fall through to `return 'style={{}}'` at line 53, silently discarding them. This needs to be replaced with a general CSS-to-JSX-style-object converter that handles arbitrary CSS properties. The function should parse the semicolon-separated CSS declarations, convert hyphenated property names to camelCase (e.g. stop-color -> stopColor), and produce a proper JSX style object like `style={{ stopColor: '#136dec', stopOpacity: 1 }}`. After fixing the converter, regenerate the affected page files so the SVG gradient stops in app/(dashboard)/performance/page.tsx lines 80-81 get proper style values. ```
--- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🚩 **All generated pages are fully static with no dynamic data or route params usage** Every generated page component (e.g., `app/(dashboard)/admin/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`, `app/(dashboard)/sem/projects/[id]/page.tsx`, `app/(dashboard)/seo/technical-audit/[clientId]/page.tsx`) uses dynamic route segments like `[id]` or `[clientId]` in their file paths, but the page components completely ignore route parameters. They render hardcoded static HTML with no `useParams()`, no data fetching, and no dynamic content. This appears intentional as a design prototype / scaffolding step, but it means these routes will show the same static content regardless of which ID is navigated to. If this is meant to be a working application rather than a prototype, these pages need to be wired up to actual data sources. --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

✅ **Resolved**: Fixed in commit 9f6d7c2: import-html.ts:92 now uses `$main('body').html()` instead of `$main.html()`, correctly extracting only the body content without the html/head/body wrapper tags.

✅ **Resolved**: Fixed in commit 9f6d7c2: The style parser now correctly converts arbitrary CSS properties to camelCase JSX. The generated output at performance/page.tsx:80-81 now has proper values like `style={{ "stopColor": "#136dec", "stopOpacity": "1" }}` instead of empty `style={{}}`.

+const fileMap = {
+    '86f2661c37764fe1beead2c45d1e4ffc_Client_Detail_Master_Page.html': 'app/(dashboard)/admin/accounts/[id]/page.tsx',
+    '0ab212f9aa314189855fde51abf893ae_SEM_Müşteri_Portföy_Yönetimi.html': 'app/(dashboard)/sem/portfolio/page.tsx',

+    '7356768a5e52481db26835175e414095_CRM_Leads_Kanban_Board.html': 'app/(dashboard)/crm/leads/page.tsx',
+    '727d3bb9ce654b5b8c1a54034b339f4b_Kapsamlı_SEO_Teknik_Denetim_Raporu.html': 'app/(dashboard)/seo/technical-audit/[clientId]/page.tsx',
+    'f68001217e014f96baf5585f5841a227_Client_Portal_Dashboard.html': 'app/client-portal/page.tsx',
body:
🔴 **Client portal page placed outside `(dashboard)` layout group, losing sidebar and header** The sidebar links to `/client-portal`, but the page file is at `app/client-portal/page.tsx` — outside the `(dashboard)` route group. All other dashboard pages are under `app/(dashboard)/...` which wraps them with the `Sidebar` and `Header` via `app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`. When a user clicks "Müşteri Portalı" in the sidebar, the navigation lands on a page rendered without any dashboard chrome (no sidebar, no header).
Root Cause The `fileMap` in `import-html.ts:14` maps the client portal HTML to `app/client-portal/page.tsx` instead of `app/(dashboard)/client-portal/page.tsx`: ```ts 'f68001217e014f96baf5585f5841a227_Client_Portal_Dashboard.html': 'app/client-portal/page.tsx', ``` All other routes are correctly nested under `app/(dashboard)/...`, e.g. `app/(dashboard)/crm/leads/page.tsx`, `app/(dashboard)/performance/page.tsx`. The `(dashboard)` layout at `app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` provides the `Sidebar` and `Header` components. Since `app/client-portal/` is a sibling of `app/(dashboard)/` rather than a child, it only gets the root layout, resulting in a bare page with no navigation. Meanwhile, `components/layout/sidebar.tsx:15` links directly to this route: ```ts { name: "Müşteri Portalı", href: "/client-portal", icon: "person" }, ``` **Impact:** Clicking "Müşteri Portalı" in the sidebar navigates the user to a page with no sidebar or header, breaking the dashboard experience and leaving the user with no way to navigate back except the browser back button.
```suggestion 'f68001217e014f96baf5585f5841a227_Client_Portal_Dashboard.html': 'app/(dashboard)/client-portal/page.tsx', ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🚩 **task-modal.tsx exports `Page` but is placed under `components/`, not a route** The file `components/tasks/task-modal.tsx` exports `default function Page()` and is structured like a page component, but it lives under `components/` rather than `app/`. This means it's not a Next.js route — it would need to be explicitly imported and rendered somewhere. The file name (`task-modal.tsx`) and the `fileMap` entry in `import-html.ts:8` suggest it's intended to be a modal component, yet it's a full page-like component with no modal behavior (no overlay, no close handling, no portal). It's currently unused — no other file imports it. --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

*(Refers to line 43)* 🚩 **Pre-existing sidebar active-state bug: root path '/' always matches** The `isActive` check at `components/layout/sidebar.tsx:43` uses `pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`)`. For the "Genel Bakış" item with `href: "/"`, this becomes `pathname.startsWith("/")` which is **always true** for every path. This means the home nav item will always appear highlighted alongside whatever page is actually active. This is pre-existing (from the initial commit) and not introduced by this PR, but the PR adds 7 new navigation items that will all visually compete with the always-active home link, making the issue more noticeable. A simple fix would be to special-case the root path: `const isActive = item.href === '/' ? pathname === '/' : (pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`));` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

body:
🟡 **Conflicting Tailwind border-color utilities make left accent border color non-deterministic** The "Acil Takip" cards use `border border-[#dbe0e6] ... border-l-4 border-red-500` (and similar patterns with `border-orange-400`, `border-primary`). Both `border-[#dbe0e6]` and `border-red-500` generate the CSS shorthand `border-color: ...` which applies to ALL four sides. One overrides the other based on stylesheet source order (not class order in HTML), meaning either all borders become red or all become gray — you cannot get a different color on just the left side this way.
Root Cause and Impact In Tailwind CSS, `border-red-500` generates `border-color: ` for all sides, and `border-[#dbe0e6]` generates `border-color: #dbe0e6` for all sides. Since both target the same CSS property, only one can win. The intended design — a gray 1px border on top/right/bottom with a colored 4px left accent — cannot be achieved with this class combination. The same pattern appears on: - Line 307: `border border-[#dbe0e6] ... border-l-4 border-orange-400` - Line 318: `border border-[#dbe0e6] ... border-l-4 border-primary` **Fix:** Use side-specific border color utilities like `border-l-red-500` instead of the shorthand `border-red-500`.
```suggestion
``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

body:
🟡 **Conflicting Tailwind border-color utilities on kanban task card override left accent color** Line 122 uses `border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700 border-l-4 border-amber-400`. The `border-amber-400` sets `border-color` for ALL sides, overriding `border-slate-200`. The intent is a slate border on three sides with an amber left accent, but one color wins for all four sides.
Root Cause and Impact Same root cause as the CRM leads page: `border-amber-400` and `border-slate-200` both generate the `border-color` shorthand for all sides. In Tailwind v4, the winner is determined by stylesheet source order, not class order in HTML. Either all borders become amber or all become slate — the mixed-color intent is lost. A similar issue exists on line 69: `border-l-4 border-primary border-t border-r border-b border-slate-200` where `border-slate-200` and `border-primary` conflict, likely causing the left accent to lose its primary color. **Fix:** Use `border-l-amber-400` (line 122) and `border-l-primary` (line 69) for side-specific colors.
```suggestion
``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🟡 **Broken `dark:` CSS class token in generated wrapper div due to regex not handling `#` in Tailwind bracket notation** The wrapper div on line 8 contains `dark: ` as a dangling, invalid CSS class: `"flex-1 flex flex-col flex-1 overflow-y-auto dark: p-6 lg:p-10 flex flex-col items-center"`. This produces a broken Tailwind class that will be passed to the DOM.
Root Cause in import-html.ts:112 The import script at `import-html.ts:112` strips background/text/dark classes from the main element's class attribute using regexes: ``` mainClass = mainClass.replace(/bg-[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]+/g, '').replace(/text-[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]+/g, '').replace(/dark:[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]+/g, ''); ``` The character class `[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]` does **not** include `#`. When the original HTML had a class like `dark:bg-[#111418]`: 1. The `bg-` regex matches only `bg-[` (stops at `#`), leaving `dark:#111418]` 2. The `dark:` regex tries to match `dark:#111418]` but `#` is the first char after `dark:` and isn't in the character class, so nothing is matched 3. After `replace(/\s+/g, ' ')` cleanup, the residual becomes `dark: ` — a broken token **Impact:** The generated page has an invalid class name fragment in its outermost div, which gets rendered into the DOM.
```suggestion
``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🟡 **Import script regex fails to strip Tailwind classes with `#` in arbitrary values (e.g. `bg-[#111418]`)** The mainClass stripping regex on line 112 uses character class `[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]` which does not include `#`, causing partial matches on Tailwind arbitrary value classes like `bg-[#111418]` or `dark:bg-[#111418]`.
Detailed Explanation When processing a class like `dark:bg-[#111418]`: - `/bg-[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]+/g` matches only `bg-[` (stops at `#`), leaving `dark:#111418]` - `/dark:[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]]+/g` fails to match `dark:#111418]` because `#` isn't in the character class This leaves broken residual tokens in the generated wrapper className. The fix should include `#`, `/`, `.`, `:`, `%`, and other characters that can appear in Tailwind's arbitrary value bracket notation. A more robust approach would be to match bracket contents explicitly, e.g. `bg-\[[^\]]*\]` or use a broader character class like `[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]#/.:%_]`. **Impact:** Any HTML source with `dark:bg-[#hex]` or similar arbitrary-value classes on the `
` element will produce broken class tokens in the generated pages.
```suggestion mainClass = mainClass.replace(/dark:[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]#/.:%_]+/g, '').replace(/bg-[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]#/.:%_]+/g, '').replace(/text-[a-zA-Z0-9-\[\]#/.:%_]+/g, ''); ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🟡 **Sidebar highlights two items simultaneously for nested `/client-portal` routes** When navigating to `/client-portal/approvals`, both "Müşteri Portalı" (`/client-portal`) and "Müşteri Onaylar" (`/client-portal/approvals`) sidebar items are highlighted as active.
Root Cause The `isActive` logic at `components/layout/sidebar.tsx:48-50` uses `pathname.startsWith(item.href + '/')` for non-root routes: ```js const isActive = item.href === '/' ? pathname === '/' : (pathname === item.href || pathname.startsWith(`${item.href}/`)); ``` When `pathname` is `/client-portal/approvals`: - For `{ href: '/client-portal' }`: `pathname.startsWith('/client-portal/')` → **true** ✗ - For `{ href: '/client-portal/approvals' }`: `pathname === '/client-portal/approvals'` → **true** ✓ Both items get `bg-primary/10 text-primary` styling, confusing the user about which page they're on. **Impact:** Visual bug — two sidebar links appear active at the same time when visiting `/client-portal/approvals`.
Prompt for agents ``` In components/layout/sidebar.tsx, the isActive logic on lines 48-50 needs to be updated to handle nested routes where one nav item's href is a prefix of another. For example, /client-portal and /client-portal/approvals. The fix should prefer exact matches and only fall back to prefix matching if no other nav item has a more specific match. One approach: check if any other navigation item's href is a longer prefix match. Another simpler approach: find the navigation item with the longest matching href and only mark that one as active. Or, change the logic to only use exact matching (pathname === item.href) which is simpler but loses sub-route highlighting for items without their own nav entry. ```
--- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

body: ✅ **Resolved**: The import-html.ts border-l regex on line 44 now converts `border-amber-400` to `border-l-amber-400`. Current sem/projects/[id]/page.tsx:123 shows `border-l-4 border-l-amber-400` (correct side-specific class), resolving the conflicting shorthand border-color issue.

🟡 **Border-left regex produces invalid `border-l-l-` Tailwind classes** The regex on line 44 is intended to convert `border-l-4 border-` to `border-l-4 border-l-`, but it also incorrectly matches cases where the border color is already `border-l-`. This produces invalid double-prefixed classes like `border-l-l-red-500`.
Root Cause and Impact The regex `border-l-(\d+)\s+border-([a-zA-Z]+(?:-\d+)?)` matches whenever `border-l-N` is followed by any `border-*`. For input like `border-l-4 border-l-red-500`, it partially matches `border-l-4 border-l` and replaces it with `border-l-4 border-l-l`, producing `border-l-4 border-l-l-red-500`. This affects 4 elements in `app/(dashboard)/sem/portfolio/page.tsx` (lines 41, 78, 164, 249), breaking their left border color styling: - `border-l-l-primary` → should be `border-l-primary` - `border-l-l-red-500` → should be `border-l-red-500` - `border-l-l-amber-500` → should be `border-l-amber-500` - `border-l-l-emerald-500` → should be `border-l-emerald-500` These invalid classes are silently ignored by Tailwind, so the colored left borders are missing entirely.
```suggestion jsx = jsx.replace(/border-l-(\d+)\s+border-(?!l-)([a-zA-Z]+(?:-\d+)?)/g, 'border-l-$1 border-l-$2'); ``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

🟡 **Invalid `border-l-l-` Tailwind classes break left border color in portfolio page** The generated file has `border-l-l-red-500` instead of `border-l-red-500`. This is an invalid Tailwind class, so the red left border color is silently dropped and not applied.
Affected Lines This issue appears on lines 41, 78, 164, and 249 of the portfolio page. For example at line 78: ``` border-l-4 border-l-l-red-500 ``` should be: ``` border-l-4 border-l-red-500 ``` The root cause is the `border-l` regex in `import-html.ts:44` which double-prefixes the `l-` when the source HTML already uses `border-l-`.
```suggestion
``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

The original scenario where `/client-portal` prefix-matched `/client-portal/approvals` causing dual highlighting can no longer occur.

body: ✅ **Resolved**: The old sidebar routes (Bordrolar /admin/payrolls, Personel Listesi /admin/employees, Vergi & SGK /admin/taxes) have been completely replaced by the new navigation structure. The sidebar no longer references these routes, and the concern about dead navigation items is resolved by the full sidebar rewrite.

🔴 **Hardcoded `ml-64` causes double sidebar offset in client portal page** The page at `app/(dashboard)/client-portal/musteri-portali-kontrol-paneli/page.tsx` has `ml-64` (margin-left: 16rem) hardcoded on its root wrapper div. Since this page is rendered inside the dashboard layout (`app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx:10-16`) which already includes a `w-64` sidebar, the `ml-64` creates an additional 256px left margin, effectively doubling the offset.
Detailed Explanation The dashboard layout at `app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` renders: ```
{children}
``` The sidebar already occupies `w-64` (256px) and the `main` element takes the remaining space via `flex-1`. Adding `ml-64` on the page content pushes it an additional 256px to the right, causing the content to be cut off on the right side or creating excessive empty space. This `ml-64` was likely present in the original standalone HTML (which had its own sidebar) and was not stripped during conversion. **Impact:** The client portal dashboard page content is shifted significantly to the right, creating a broken layout.
```suggestion
``` --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

body:
🟡 **Sidebar `isGroupActive` check fails for "Genel Bakış" because `items` is an empty array** In the sidebar navigation, the `isGroupActive` check at `components/layout/sidebar.tsx:132` calls `group.items.some(...)` on every group. For "Genel Bakış" (which has `items: []`), `some()` on an empty array always returns `false`, so `isGroupActive` is always `false` for this group. This means the "Genel Bakış" link never visually appears as the active group based on the group-level highlighting logic.
Root Cause The code at line 132: ```js const isGroupActive = group.items.some(i => i.href === '/' ? pathname === '/' : ...); ``` For "Genel Bakış" with `items: []`, `some()` returns `false`. However, the actual active state for the link is calculated separately at line 136 (`const isActive = pathname === '/'`), so the link itself does get highlighted correctly. The `isGroupActive` variable is only used for collapsed group highlighting (line 164), which doesn't apply to "Genel Bakış" since it enters the early return branch. So in practice the visual result is correct, but the logic is semantically inconsistent. **Impact:** No visible user-facing impact since the early return branch at line 135 handles "Genel Bakış" separately with its own `isActive` check. This is a code quality issue rather than a functional bug.
--- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*

body: 🚩 **Several pages that existed before this PR (like /admin/accounts/[id], /admin/dashboard, /crm/leads, etc.) are not in the sidebar navigation** The PR adds many new routes via the import script, but the sidebar navigation in `components/layout/sidebar.tsx` does not include entries for several pages that were also added in this PR but aren't from the import script's `fileMap`. For example, routes like `/admin/accounts/[id]`, `/admin/dashboard`, `/client-portal/approvals`, `/client-portal/page`, `/crm/leads`, `/performance/page`, `/sem/portfolio`, `/sem/projects/[id]`, `/seo/rank-tracking`, `/seo/technical-analysis`, `/seo/technical-audit/[clientId]`, and `/social-media/approval-center` have page components but no sidebar links. Users cannot navigate to these pages through the UI. This may be intentional (accessed via other in-page links) or an oversight. --- *Was this helpful? React with 👍 or 👎 to provide feedback.*
